frame_analyses.description: Title slide for Stanford FLS suturing tutorial
External view showing proper suture grasping technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates grasping suture at stiffest part within 1cm of needle

frame_analyses.description: Shows needle stabilization at point 2 with Penrose drain setup
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good demonstration of proper needle stabilization technique

frame_analyses.description: Challenge demonstration of loading needle in mid-air
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows advanced technique for needle loading without surface contact

frame_analyses.description: Task parameters slide showing 15cm suture, 10 minute max time, 112 second target
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Reference information for FLS Task 5 requirements

frame_analyses.description: Right hand turning needle clockwise to create rainbow pattern for surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper technique for first throw of surgeon's knot with double wrap

frame_analyses.description: Shows 2 wraps clockwise creating 'under over' pattern for surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear visualization of double throw technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Pulling suture ends in opposite directions to cinch down the surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper tensioning technique to secure the double throw

frame_analyses.description: Wrapping with small circles near instrument tips for second throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows technique for single throw after hand switching

knot_assessments.notes: Demonstrates proper surgeon's knot with double wrap clockwise

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Cannot assess as this is instructional video, not actual performance

technique_summary: This is an instructional video demonstrating proper FLS Task 5 technique, not an actual performance to be scored. Shows excellent teaching of surgeon's knot formation and proper hand techniques.
improvement_suggestions: This is a tutorial video, not a performance assessment
strengths: Excellent demonstration of proper surgeon's knot technique
Clear visualization of double wrap formation
Good teaching of hand positioning and suture manipulation
